inheritance-for-shared-behavior.label: Inheritance for Shared Behavior
inheritance-for-shared-behavior.summary: Move shared setup into a CatalogItem base class, then create a Book subclass that inherits attributes and methods without rewriting them.

try_inheritance_for_shared_behavior_sket.title: Build the base class
try_inheritance_for_shared_behavior_sket.prompt: Create the shared `CatalogItem` base class in `models/catalog_item.py`. Store `title` in `__init__`, then make `label()` return the title. In `main.py`, use the provided object `CatalogItem("Hidden Figures")` and print its label.
try_inheritance_for_shared_behavior_sket.hint: Put the shared title setup in the base class first.

help.concept: A base class holds behavior that related subclasses can reuse later.
help.hint_1: The constructor should save the incoming `title` on `self`.
help.hint_2: The `label()` method should return that stored title.

try_inheritance_for_shared_behavior_sket.starterCode:
from models.catalog_item import CatalogItem

item = CatalogItem("Hidden Figures")
print(item.label())

main_py.content:
from models.catalog_item import CatalogItem

item = CatalogItem("Hidden Figures")
print(item.label())

try_inheritance_for_shared_behavior_sket.solutionCode:
from models.catalog_item import CatalogItem

item = CatalogItem("Hidden Figures")
print(item.label())

main_py.content:
from models.catalog_item import CatalogItem

item = CatalogItem("Hidden Figures")
print(item.label())

2.message: Store the title on each CatalogItem object.

3.message: label() should return the stored title.

try_inheritance_for_shared_behavior_sket.title: Create the Book subclass
try_inheritance_for_shared_behavior_sket.prompt: Use the provided `CatalogItem` base class and create a subclass shell. In `models/book.py`, make `Book` inherit from `CatalogItem` with `pass`. In `main.py`, use the provided `Book("The Martian")` object and print `book.title`.
try_inheritance_for_shared_behavior_sket.hint: The subclass only needs the base class name in parentheses.

help.concept: A subclass can begin as a small shell and still inherit the base constructor.
help.hint_2: Keep `pass` inside the class body for now.

try_inheritance_for_shared_behavior_sket.starterCode:
from models.book import Book

book = Book("The Martian")
print(book.title)

main_py.content:
from models.book import Book

book = Book("The Martian")
print(book.title)

try_inheritance_for_shared_behavior_sket.solutionCode:
from models.book import Book

book = Book("The Martian")
print(book.title)

main_py.content:
from models.book import Book

book = Book("The Martian")
print(book.title)

1.message: Book should be constructible with one title.

2.message: A Book object should have the inherited title attribute.

try_inheritance_for_shared_behavior_sket.title: Use the inherited label method
try_inheritance_for_shared_behavior_sket.prompt: Keep the same `CatalogItem` and `Book` classes. Do not write a new method in `Book`. In `main.py`, use the provided `Book("The Hobbit")` object and print the inherited `label()` result.
try_inheritance_for_shared_behavior_sket.hint: Call the method that already exists on the base class.

help.concept: Subclass objects can use base-class methods directly.
help.hint_2: If inheritance is set up correctly, `Book` does not need its own `label()` here.

try_inheritance_for_shared_behavior_sket.starterCode:
from models.book import Book

book = Book("The Hobbit")
print(book.label())

main_py.content:
from models.book import Book

book = Book("The Hobbit")
print(book.label())

try_inheritance_for_shared_behavior_sket.solutionCode:
from models.book import Book

book = Book("The Hobbit")
print(book.label())

main_py.content:
from models.book import Book

book = Book("The Hobbit")
print(book.label())

1.message: Create the Book object in main.py.

sc-inheritance-shared-work.title: Choose why a base class helps here
sc-inheritance-shared-work.prompt: Book and Movie both need the same `title` setup. Why is a base class helpful?
sc-inheritance-shared-work.hint: Pick the answer about reusing shared code.

help.concept: Inheritance is helpful when related classes share the same core behavior.
help.hint_1: Look for the choice about storing shared setup once.
help.hint_2: Avoid answers about unrelated classes or identical output forever.

options.a: It prevents any subclass from having its own behavior.
options.b: It stores shared setup once so related subclasses can reuse it.
options.c: It turns every object into the same subclass.
options.d: It removes the need to create objects.

fb-inheritance-subclass-name.title: Complete the parent class name
fb-inheritance-subclass-name.prompt: Fill in the base class name: `class Book([blank1]):`
fb-inheritance-subclass-name.hint: Use the shared class that stores `title`.

help.concept: A subclass names its parent inside the parentheses.
help.hint_1: The base class in this topic is the shared item class.
help.hint_2: Use the exact class name from the lesson.

dr-inheritance-build-order.title: Order the inheritance steps
dr-inheritance-build-order.prompt: Put these steps in a sensible beginner order.
dr-inheritance-build-order.hint: Start with the base class, then connect the subclass, then use it.

help.concept: A clean inheritance setup starts with the shared base class before the subclass uses it.
help.hint_1: The base class must exist before the subclass can inherit from it.
help.hint_2: Using the inherited method happens after the objects exist.

sc-inheritance-method-reuse.title: Spot the inherited behavior
sc-inheritance-method-reuse.prompt: If `Book` inherits from `CatalogItem`, what can happen even when `Book` only contains `pass`?
sc-inheritance-method-reuse.hint: Think about what the base class already provides.

help.concept: A subclass can still use methods and attributes inherited from its base class.
help.hint_1: The base class already defines the shared behavior.
help.hint_2: The answer is about reuse, not copying code by hand.

options.a: A Book object can still use `label()` from CatalogItem.
options.b: A Book object loses access to the title attribute.
options.c: A Book object must redefine every inherited method before running.
options.d: A Book object cannot be created until it overrides `label()`.

sketch-0.title: Why shared setup belongs in one base class

sketch-1.title: A subclass can start as a simple shell

sketch-2.title: Inherited methods work on subclass objects
